{{Quote|See that little one I'm holding? His name was N'a-kee-tula, which means 'sweetheart.' He was so full of life—all those kids were.|Padmé Amidala, explaining a holograph featuring her and N'a-kee-tula to Anakin Skywalker|<ref name="AOTC novel" />}}
'''N'a-kee-tula''' was a [[Shadda-Bi-Boran (species)|Shadda-Bi-Boran]] [[Youngling/Legends|child]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Shadda-Bi-Boran/Legends|Shadda-Bi-Boran]]. In [[38 BBY/Legends|38 BBY]], the Shadda-Bi-Borans were [[Shadda-Bi-Boran exodus|evacuated]] from their dying [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] by the [[Refugee Relief Movement/Legends|Refugee Relief Movement]], including the [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padme Naberrie]], who felt N'a-kee-tula was a special child. N'a-kee-tula ultimately [[Death/Legends|died]], along with the rest of his species, as they were unable to adapt to life on other planets.

==Biography==
{{Quote|They were never able to adapt, they were never able to live off their native planet.|Padmé Amidala explains the fate of Shadda-Bi-Boran to Anakin Skywalker|<ref name="AOTC novel" />}}
[[File:Shaddabiboran_TEA.png|thumb|righy|250px|Shadda-Bi-Boran, N'a-kee-tula's doomed homeworld]]
N'a-kee-tula was a [[Shadda-Bi-Boran (species)|Shadda-Bi-Boran]] [[Youngling/Legends|child]] who lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Shadda-Bi-Boran/Legends|Shadda-Bi-Boran]].<ref name="Characters" /> His name meant "sweetheart" in the [[Shadda-Bi]] [[Language/Legends|language]].<ref name="AOTC novel" /> The Shadda-Bi-Boran [[Species/Legends|species]] relied on a [[Poison/Legends|toxin]]-purifying enzyme that was only activated by the light of the [[Star/Legends|sun]] [[Shadda (star)|Shadda]],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> which, at the [[Time/Legends|time]] of N'a-kee-tula's childhood,<ref name="AOTC novel" /> had entered a [[White dwarf/Legends|white dwarf]] phase<ref name="TEA" /> and was about to implode,<ref name="Characters" /> which would cause the planet to freeze over.<ref name="TEA" />

In [[38 BBY/Legends|38 BBY]],<ref name="Death" /> as the planet's demise began, members of the [[Refugee Relief Movement/Legends|Refugee Relief Movement]] arrived to attempt to [[Shadda-Bi-Boran exodus|evacuate]] the population to [[Star system/Legends|systems]] with stars that they hoped would have the same purifying effect on the Shadda-Bi-Borans as Shadda.<ref name="TEA" /> One of the volunteers in relocating the planet's children<ref name="AOTC novel" /> was the seven-[[Standard year/Legends|year]]-old [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Naberrie]], who bonded with N'a-kee-tula and believed he was a special child.<ref name="Characters" /> At one point during the evacuation, Naberrie, N'a-kee-tula, and dozens of Shadda-Bi-Boran posed for a [[holograph]] recording<ref name="AOTC novel" /> where Amidala and N'a-kee-tula rocked back and forth together.<ref name="AOTC" />

N'a-kee-tula was unable to survive away from his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and [[Death/Legends|perished]], as did the rest of his species. Naberrie kept the holograph of her and N'a-kee-tula at her [[Naberrie family/Legends|family]] [[Naberrie family home|home]] on the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="AOTC novel" /> In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="TEA" /> Naberrie,<ref name="AOTC novel" /> whose surname by then was Amidala after her service as [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|Queen of Naboo]],<ref name="Characters" /> was packing [[Clothing/Legends|clothes]] in her room along with the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]. He enquired about the holograph, and Amidala explained her time in the Refugee Relief Movement and spoke somberly of N'a-kee-tula's memory.<ref name="AOTC novel" />

==Personality and traits==
Naberrie described N'a-kee-tula as full of life, like the other Shadda-Bi-Boran children. He had [[Color/Legends|green]] skin<ref name="AOTC novel" /> and black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="AOTC" />

==Equipment==
During the recording of the holograph with Naberrie, N'a-kee-tula wore a yellow shirt.<ref name="AOTC" />

==Behind the scenes==
N'a-kee-tula was originally pictured in a holograph in a [[Content cut from Star Wars|deleted scene]] in the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], written and directed by [[George Lucas]].<ref name="AOTC" /> His holograph was featured in the film's [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones (novelization)|novelization]] written by [[R. A. Salvatore]]<ref name="AOTC novel" /> and in the illustrated version of the film's screenplay included in ''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode II: Attack of the Clones]]'',<ref name="Art">''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode II: Attack of the Clones]]''</ref> both of which were published on [[April 23]], [[2002]],<ref name="AOTCNovelDate">{{RandomHouseOld|url=author/results.pperl?authorid=26771|text=R.A. Salvatore}}</ref> prior to the film's premiere on [[May 16]] later that year.<ref name="Year">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref>

The deleted scene featuring N'a-kee-tula's holograph, and thus the first visual depiction of the character,<ref name="AOTC" /> was included on the DVD release of ''Attack of the Clones'', which was released on [[November 12]], 2002.<ref name="DVD">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/news/2002/08/news20020801.html|text=Episode II On DVD & VHS}}</ref> The deleted scene depicts only two Shadda-Bi-Borans in the holograph, and although Amidala's dialogue refers to N'a-kee-tula as the one she is holding, in the image she has her [[Limb/Legends|arms]] around both children, leaving it unclear which one is N'a-kee-tula.<ref name="AOTC" /> The novelization describes dozens of children participating in the holograph,<ref name="AOTC novel" /> and this article follows the novel's portrayal.
